title: RFK Jr. Appears Disengaged on Many Health Department Matters Beyond Vaccines
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has demonstrated little interest in managing his sprawling department as he focuses on food and vaccine policies, according to colleagues.

title: An Uncertain Win for Immigrants Seeking to Stay in U.S.
summary: After a judge’s ruling, there was a sense of renewed hope that immigration applications that were put on hold would move forward. But how soon that would happen was unknown.

title: Hegseth Criticizes Europe Over Migration ‘Invasion’ in D-Day Speech
summary: The remarks from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reflect many of the Trump administration’s previous assertions on immigrants in Europe, which overlap with the language of European far-right political parties.

title: Kennedy Center Loses Case Against Musician Who Canceled Over Trump Naming Dispute
summary: A judge sided with jazz performer Chuck Redd, who canceled a 2025 holiday concert after President Trump’s name was added to the building.

title: Trump Says Iran Has Made a ‘Big’ Nuclear Promise. It Isn’t New.
summary: President Trump’s boasts of securing a commitment from Iranian leaders not to develop a nuclear weapon have puzzled nuclear experts who note that Tehran has made that pledge for more than 50 years.

title: Trump’s Defense Department Sees Growing Espionage Threat From Israel
summary: The Defense Department has increased the counterintelligence threat assessment to its highest level, and Israel is believed to have eavesdropped on American negotiations with Iran.

title: Gunman Kills at Least One in Attack on Central Israel
summary: Israeli authorities described the shootings as a terrorist attack. Hamas, the Palestinian militant group, praised the assault, but did not claim responsibility.

title: As Oil Prices Spike, Talk of ‘Demand Destruction’ Sets In
summary: The decades-old term refers to the sustained loss of demand for a commodity, caused by high prices.

title: Scott Pelley on the Bari Weiss Era and His Last Days at ‘60 Minutes’
summary: Lulu Garcia-Navarro sits down for an exclusive interview with the now-former CBS News correspondent.

title: Trump Looms Over Pivotal Republican Senate Runoff in Georgia
summary: The president has not yet endorsed Representative Mike Collins or Derek Dooley, a former football coach, in the race to challenge the Democratic senator, Jon Ossoff.

title: South Carolina Governor’s Race Tests the Strength of a Trump Endorsement
summary: The Republican candidates for South Carolina governor went to extraordinary lengths for the president’s support. But his choice of Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette has not quite sealed the deal among voters.

title: The Bidens Return With New Book and South Dakota Speech Targeting Trump
summary: The re-emergence of the former first family has been fraught for some Democrats who just want to move on.

title: Christine Hunsicker, CaaStle CEO, Confessed to Fraud. The Board Let Her Stay in Charge.
summary: The final months of the fashion tech start-up CaaStle included fake audits, stock buybacks and a damage control strategy led by the chief executive who defrauded it.

title: 2026 Tony Awards: What to Expect
summary: This year’s annual celebration of the best on Broadway is being hosted by Pink.

title: June Squibb, the ‘Foulest Mouth on Broadway’, Is Up for a Tony at 96
summary: At 96, June Squibb is one of the oldest acting nominees in the history of the Tony Awards. Back in the 1960s, she was known for an entirely different distinction.

title: How to Watch the 2026 Tony Awards
summary: The ceremony, at Radio City Music Hall, will be broadcast on CBS starting at 8 p.m. Eastern, and livestreamed on Paramount+ for premium subscribers.

title: Americans Love Soccer Stars Who Aren’t Made in America
summary: The United States has gone wild over foreign-born players on several occasions. Now the largest World Cup ever is arriving.

title: Ahead of World Cup, Two Generations of Haitian Players Unite
summary: A player from Haiti’s 1974 World Cup team, the last to qualify for the tournament, met with the co-captain of this year’s squad in South Florida.

title: Why We Still Need Steven Spielberg
summary: Hollywood is struggling, but Spielberg insists that the big screen is still the best place to work out our collective dreams, fears, joys and sorrows.

title: Who Will Account for Another Foreign Policy Failure?
summary: The American foreign policy class should shed its addiction to military force.

title: The Betrayal of Black Voters Threatens Our Democracy
summary: The sweeping effort to dismantle Black-majority congressional districts in the South will have far-reaching consequences for all Americans, and for our democracy.

title: Touching Grass
summary: There’s no better time than early summer to put away your phone and get some fresh air. We have a challenge to help you actually do it.

title: A New Governor Faces Her First Crisis Amid Tear Gas and Tough Decisions
summary: Gov. Mikie Sherrill dispatched the New Jersey State Police outside the Delaney Hall migrant detention center after federal officials said they would flood the area with armed agents. Not everyone approved.
